req.title: Create encounter (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
preconditions: role:clinician
patient exists

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-056 mandates that the /Encounter endpoint accepts valid start/end pairs and rejects end-before-start payloads with HTTP 422. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive — a well-formed encounter with start < end returns 201; (2) NEG — end strictly before start returns 422; (3) BVA — end equals start (zero-duration boundary) to probe the inclusive/exclusive edge; (4) NEG/DT — missing mandatory 'subject' field returns 422; (5) NEG — a non-clinician role (patient) is denied with 403. Together these cover the equivalence partitions (valid / invalid timestamp order / missing fields / wrong role), the boundary between valid and invalid timestamps, and the permission denial negative path.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps
preconditions: Authenticated as role:clinician
Patient resource exists with known patient_id
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter with resourceType=Encounter, status=in-progress, subject.reference pointing to existing patient, valid period where start < end
trace: R-HLT-056: clinician creates encounter with valid timestamps -> encounter stored

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated as role:clinician
Patient resource exists with known patient_id
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter with period.end set to one hour before period.start
expected: Response body contains an error/issue indicating invalid period
trace: R-HLT-056: Error pathway — end < start must return 422

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated as role:clinician
Patient resource exists with known patient_id
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter with period.end set to the exact same instant as period.start (zero-duration encounter)
expected: HTTP 201 Created if zero-duration is treated as valid, OR HTTP 422 if end==start is rejected
Response status code is one of [201, 422] — not 500
Whichever branch is taken is consistent across repeated identical calls (idempotent classification)
trace: R-HLT-056: BVA on period boundary — end == start is the immediate adjacent value to the invalid partition

tests.name: test_create_encounter_missing_subject_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated as role:clinician
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter with a valid period but with the mandatory 'subject' field omitted entirely
expected: Response body references missing mandatory element 'subject'
trace: R-HLT-056: Negative path — missing mandatory elements must be rejected

tests.name: test_create_encounter_non_clinician_role_denied
preconditions: Authenticated as role:patient (not clinician)
Patient resource exists with known patient_id
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter with a fully valid payload using a patient-role bearer token
trace: R-HLT-056: Negative path — permission denial; only role:clinician may create encounters

tests.name: test_create_encounter_duplicate_active_state_transition
preconditions: Authenticated as role:clinician
Patient resource exists with known patient_id
An active (in-progress) encounter already exists for the patient
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Encounter again for the same patient with status=in-progress while one is already active
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict OR HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity indicating conflicting state transition
No duplicate active encounter is created
trace: R-HLT-056: Negative path — conflicting state transitions must be tested
